revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:番山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.850916666667|N|135.58106388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}}}}
'''Banyama Kofun''' (番山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|qid=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a [[scallop-shaped kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳) and remains unprotected as a designated historic site. == Overview == The Banyama Kofun was constructed on a terrace in the northern region of Osaka Prefecture.<ref name="description">Site description panel, Takatsuki City Board of Education, updated 2017.</ref> Although the mound itself has not been directly excavated, surrounding areas have been subject to several archaeological surveys. The mound has a scallop-shaped plan with a short front portion facing southwest, which is now mostly destroyed, giving it the appearance of a [[round Kofun]]. The remaining rear mound measures 56 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.<ref name="description" /> The surface of the mound has revealed traces of a [[cylindrical haniwa]] row as well as house-shaped haniwa.<ref name="kofun">{{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref=Harvard|Banyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}</ref> A surrounding moat was also identified. The burial chamber and other structural details remain unclear. The mound is estimated to have been built during the middle [[Kofun period]] in the 5th century, possibly in the latter half or near its end.<ref name="description" /><ref name="heibonsha">{{Cite book|title=Osaka Prefecture Place Names: Japan Historical Place Name Series|year=1986|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref=Harvard|Banyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986|qq=}}</ref> The mound's construction is thought to share the same design scheme as the nearby [[Maetsuka Kofun]] in Takatsuki.<ref name="heibonsha" /> The haniwa are believed to have been produced at the [[Shinike Haniwa Production Site]], a historic site located in the Kamitsuchimuro area of Takatsuki.<ref name="heibonsha" /> == Key Features == * '''Shape:''' Scallop-shaped kofun with a short front portion now destroyed. * '''Size:''' Rear mound diameter of 56 meters, height of 7 meters. * '''Artifacts:''' Cylindrical and house-shaped haniwa identified on the mound's surface. * '''Surrounding Moat:''' Evidence of a moat encircling the mound. == Significance == Although not a protected historic site, Banyama Kofun provides important insight into burial practices and the socio-political structure of the region during the 5th century. Its connection to the Maetsuka Kofun and the Shinike Haniwa Production Site suggests coordinated efforts in kofun construction and artifact production. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref=Harvard|Banyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}} * {{Cite book|title=Osaka Prefecture Place Names: Japan Historical Place Name Series|year=1986|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref=Harvard|Banyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986|qq=}} == Related Articles == * [[Maetsuka Kofun|Maetsuka Kofun (Takatsuki)]] * [[Shinike Haniwa Production Site]] {{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|番山古墳|version=92888207|comment=Imported full ja history}}
